theme_and_archetype.description: A high-craft, reading-focused editorial experience designed for founders. Blends Archetype 4 (Swiss clarity) with Archetype 1 (Organic paper warmth). It feels like reading a premium broadsheet like the Financial Times or a Morning Brew editorial, structured in a modern app like Arc or Linear.

heading.description: Striking, variable-weight serif for major headlines and mastheads. Gives the artifact a distinct, classic-yet-modern editorial edge.

editorial_body.description: High-legibility serif specifically crafted for continuous on-screen reading. Used for the actual briefing content.

ui_sans.description: Sharp, geometric, modern sans for datelines, UI controls, right-panel metadata, and the Demo Console.

layout_and_spacing.strategy: Single-column reading experience with a right-side functional companion panel.

grid.main_reading_column: flex-1 flex justify-center px-4 sm:px-8 py-16 md:py-24 overflow-y-auto
grid.right_companion_panel: hidden lg:flex w-80 lg:w-96 flex-col border-l border-border bg-card/50 backdrop-blur-xl h-screen sticky top-0 px-6 py-8

newspaper_elements.drop_cap: float-left text-6xl leading-[0.8] pr-2 pt-2 font-heading font-light

motion_and_interactions.global: Subtle, refined micro-interactions. No bouncing or spring-heavy physics.
motion_and_interactions.reading_sections: Staggered fade-up on scroll (y: 10, opacity: 0 to 1, duration: 0.6s).

cinematic_regeneration.animation: Screen dims to bg-black/80 backdrop-blur-md, abstract texture overlay fades in. Text scrambles/decodes into place using a monospace/sans swap effect before the new brief settles.

morning_brief.description: The main content area. Broken into 4 distinct editorial sections.
sections: One Thing Today (Hero insight, uses drop cap)
Three to Handle Personally (Bulleted/numbered list with generous spacing)
One to Cancel (Strikethrough styling or muted blockquote)
One to Revive (Highlighted or indented emphasis)

daily_companion.description: Right side panel showing AI context states.

states.name: Silent
states.visual: Muted text, minimal border, no background.

states.name: Attention Required
states.visual: Subtle yellow/amber left border, bolded title.

states.name: Escalation
states.visual: Solid background in light orange/red tint, dark text, pulsing indicator dot.

states.name: Decision Needed
states.visual: Interactive block with inline 'Approve/Decline' ghost buttons.

evening_close.layout: Takes over the main column. Minimalist. One question at a time vertically centered.
evening_close.input: Naked input field (border-b only) or large text-area with no visible border until focused. Uses the Serif reading font for input text.

demo_console.description: Hidden judge palette triggered via Cmd+K.
demo_console.style: Glassmorphism. backdrop-blur-2xl bg-black/60 (dark mode forced). Sharp 1px white/10 border. Uses Manrope font exclusively.

founder_avatar.description: Priya Mehrotra demo persona avatar (Confident Indian female founder in a professional setting).
founder_avatar.usage: Top right of the Daily Companion panel.

abstract_texture.usage: Use as a fixed background overlay with mix-blend-multiply (light mode) or opacity-5 (dark mode) to give the 'artifact/paper' feel.

testing.requirements: All interactive elements, buttons, and form inputs MUST include `data-testid` attributes (e.g., `data-testid='regenerate-brief-button'`).

instructions_to_main_agent: Prioritize HTML semantics and typography hierarchy over complex component nesting.
Use standard Tailwind CSS combined with custom font configurations in tailwind.config.js for Fraunces, Newsreader, and Manrope.
Do NOT use standard Shadcn cards for the main Morning Brief; build it with raw divs and typographic rules to maintain the editorial newspaper look.
For the right-side Daily Companion, you may use heavily customized Shadcn components (like ScrollArea or subtle Separators).
Implement the 'Imagine this in Slack' and 'Imagine this in Email' as static mockup overlays or dedicated sub-pages with realistic structural borders.
Apply `max-w-[640px]` strictly to the brief reading column to ensure optimal line lengths (65-75 characters).
